diff:
diff --git a/context.go b/context.go
index 8222073..28da1e8 100644
--- a/context.go
+++ b/context.go
@@ -76,6 +76,12 @@ type Context struct {
 
 	methodsAllowed   []methodTyp // allowed methods in case of a 405
 	methodNotAllowed bool
+
+	// methodNotAllowedParams captures the URL parameter keys and values at
+	// the point where a route was found but the requested method was not
+	// allowed. These are used to populate URLParams so that URLParam() works
+	// correctly inside MethodNotAllowed handlers.
+	methodNotAllowedParams RouteParams
 }
 
 // Reset a routing context to its initial state.
@@ -92,6 +98,8 @@ func (x *Context) Reset() {
 	x.routeParams.Values = x.routeParams.Values[:0]
 	x.methodNotAllowed = false
 	x.methodsAllowed = x.methodsAllowed[:0]
+	x.methodNotAllowedParams.Keys = x.methodNotAllowedParams.Keys[:0]
+	x.methodNotAllowedParams.Values = x.methodNotAllowedParams.Values[:0]
 	x.parentCtx = nil
 }

diff --git a/tree.go b/tree.go
index 95f31d4..165e25b 100644
--- a/tree.go
+++ b/tree.go
@@ -380,6 +380,12 @@ func (n *node) FindRoute(rctx *Context, method methodTyp, path string) (*node, e
 	// Find the routing handlers for the path
 	rn := n.findRoute(rctx, method, path)
 	if rn == nil {
+		// When methodNotAllowed is set, commit the captured param keys/values
+		// to URLParams so that URLParam() works in MethodNotAllowed handlers.
+		if rctx.methodNotAllowed && len(rctx.methodNotAllowedParams.Keys) > 0 {
+			rctx.URLParams.Keys = append(rctx.URLParams.Keys, rctx.methodNotAllowedParams.Keys...)
+			rctx.URLParams.Values = append(rctx.URLParams.Values, rctx.methodNotAllowedParams.Values...)
+		}
 		return nil, nil, nil
 	}
+
+	// plus snapshot code in findRoute at the two methodNotAllowed detection sites

excluded_defs.name: URLParam
excluded_defs.reason: read to understand how URL params are accessed, confirms the fix needs to populate URLParams.Keys/Values, not routeParams

exploration_log.aha_moment: routeParams is reset on backtracking but needs to survive long enough for FindRoute to commit to URLParams. A snapshot in Context.methodNotAllowedParams (taken at detection time) survives the backtrack and can be committed in FindRoute.

dead_ends.explored: Adding to routeParams.Keys directly when methodNotAllowed is set
dead_ends.why_irrelevant: routeParams.Keys is NOT reset during backtracking (only Values is), causing dirty key accumulation that led to a URLParams keys/values length mismatch and a panic in TestMuxRegexp3.

exploration_log.hindsight: Should have traced the backtracking reset logic before adding to routeParams.Keys; the asymmetry between Keys/Values reset was the critical insight.

alternatives: Reset routeParams.Keys alongside Values during backtrack
key_decisions.decision: Add a methodNotAllowedParams snapshot field to Context and populate it at detection time
key_decisions.reasoning: Direct routeParams.Keys mutation is unsafe because backtracking only resets Values. Resetting Keys along with Values would require tracking prevlenKeys at many sites. The snapshot approach is cleanest: save at detection time before any reset, commit in FindRoute.

search_sequence.reasoning: Task pointed to findRoute as the location of the bug.
search_sequence.result: Found two methodNotAllowed detection sites (one inside the ntParam loop around line 461, one in the outer xsearch==0 check around line 518). Neither commits paramKeys when setting methodNotAllowed.

search_sequence.action: Read context.go Context struct and Reset()
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to understand the URLParams/routeParams relationship and what gets reset.
search_sequence.result: routeParams is reset at the start of each FindRoute call. URLParams accumulates across sub-routers. routeParams is committed to URLParams only when rn != nil.

search_sequence.action: First attempt: add to routeParams.Keys at methodNotAllowed sites
search_sequence.reasoning: Task says to commit to routeParams.Keys.
search_sequence.result: TestMuxRegexp3 panicked with index out of range — routeParams.Keys got dirty from a failed branch, then when the correct branch was found, the keys count exceeded the values count.

search_sequence.action: Traced the panic: subrouter with two param patterns sharing a path prefix
search_sequence.reasoning: Understanding why keys/values went out of sync.
search_sequence.result: The {dns} node set methodNotAllowed, my code added 'dns' to routeParams.Keys, then {id} found the route and added 'id' — two keys, one value = panic.

solve_notes: Added a `methodNotAllowedParams` snapshot field to `Context` (populated in the two `methodNotAllowed = true` sites inside `findRoute` using the current `routeParams.Values` at that point) and modified `FindRoute` to commit it to `URLParams` when `rn == nil` but `methodNotAllowed` is true. A direct `routeParams.Keys` approach failed because backtracking only resets `routeParams.Values`, not `Keys`, causing dirty-key panics in tests with multiple param-node branches.
task_text:
Fix URLParam returning empty string in MethodNotAllowed handler

When a route is defined as `r.Get("/users/{id}", handler)` and a
request arrives with an unsupported HTTP method (e.g., DELETE), the
`MethodNotAllowedHandler` is invoked. Inside that handler,
`chi.URLParam(r, "id")` returns an empty string even though the URL
clearly matched the parameter pattern. The cause is in `tree.go`'s
`FindRoute`: during tree traversal, parameter values are appended to
`rctx.routeParams.Values`, but parameter keys from `h.paramKeys` are
only committed to `rctx.routeParams.Keys` when a handler node is
matched for the requested method. For method-not-allowed cases, the
keys are never committed, leaving `URLParams.Keys` empty while
`URLParams.Values` contains the captured values. Fix `findRoute` in
`tree.go` to also commit param keys to `rctx.routeParams.Keys` when
setting `rctx.methodNotAllowed = true`.

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: exercises FindRoute via router dispatch, covers the modified nil-return branch
tests nested subrouter method-not-allowed cases which exercise the two methodNotAllowed detection sites in findRoute

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that verify the behavior of FindRoute, findRoute, methodNotAllowed in tree.go and context.go

thrash_preventing_defs.name: URLParam
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows that URLParam reads from URLParams.Keys/Values (not routeParams), which determines the correct mechanism for the fix

thrash_preventing_defs.name: routeHTTP
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows how FindRoute result is used — URLParams is iterated only when h != nil, so the MethodNotAllowed path needs a different population mechanism

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set covers the four edited definitions. An AI agent would also read URLParam (context.go) to understand that the fix must target URLParams.Keys/Values rather than routeParams.Keys (which is an internal field that doesn't survive FindRoute's nil return), and routeHTTP (mux.go) to see the exact execution path for method-not-allowed — without these two reads, the agent would likely implement the fix in routeParams incorrectly and need to backtrack after seeing the test failure.
